user_query:
I need an automated local audit of the included AngularJS + Bootstrap sample app. Please implement a script or set of shell commands that operate entirely on the provided workspace and produce both a machine-readable report and a config update.

Inputs (already in the workspace):
- Source tree under ./app
  - ./app/app.module.js
  - ./app/components/navbar/navbar.directive.js
  - ./app/components/main/main.controller.js
  - ./app/components/buttons/btnGroup.directive.js
  - ./app/templates/navbar.html
  - ./app/templates/main.html
  - ./app/templates/buttons.html
- Config at ./config/app.config.json

Required behavior:
1) Inspect files and directories to discover AngularJS artifacts and templates:
   - Read ./app/app.module.js to extract the AngularJS module name (from angular.module('<name>', ...)).
   - Recursively scan ./app for .js files to extract:
     - All controller names defined via .controller('Name', ...), capturing the controller name and its file path.
     - All directive names defined via .directive('name', ...), capturing the directive name, its file path, and templateUrl if present (normalize to a relative forward-slash path as written in the code, or null if absent).
   - Scan all HTML files under ./app/templates to extract Bootstrap CSS class tokens from class="..." attributes. Only include tokens whose value begins with any of these prefixes: btn, navbar, container, row, col-, alert, label, badge, dropdown, list-group, form-control, input-group, panel, card. Deduplicate per file and sort alphabetically within each file’s list.

2) Produce a structured report at ./output/bootstrap_audit.json with this schema:
   {
     "module": string,
     "controllers": [ { "name": string, "file": string } ],
     "directives": [ { "name": string, "file": string, "templateUrl": string|null } ],
     "templates": [ { "file": string, "bootstrap_classes": [string], "used_by": [string] } ],
     "totals": { "controllers": number, "directives": number, "templates": number, "unique_bootstrap_classes": number }
   }
   - controllers: every discovered controller with its source file path relative to the workspace, using forward slashes.
   - directives: every discovered directive with its source file path and its templateUrl if present; use null when no templateUrl is defined.
   - templates: one entry per HTML file under ./app/templates with bootstrap_classes as described above and used_by listing directive names whose templateUrl matches this template’s relative path exactly.
   - totals.unique_bootstrap_classes is the count of distinct class tokens (per the prefix rule) across ALL templates combined.
   - Do not hardcode any values; compute everything by analyzing the files.

3) Modify configuration in place at ./config/app.config.json by adding a top-level key "bootstrapAudit" with the following fields (leave all existing keys unchanged):
   - reportPath: "output/bootstrap_audit.json"
   - uniqueClassCount: must equal the number in report.totals.unique_bootstrap_classes
   - lastUpdated: an ISO-8601 timestamp string for when the report was generated

Additional constraints:
- Do not rename or move any existing files. Create the ./output directory if it does not exist.
- All paths in the JSON report must be relative to the workspace and use forward slashes.
- The script should be idempotent: re-running it updates the report and the bootstrapAudit section accordingly.

Deliverables:
- ./output/bootstrap_audit.json created as specified
- ./config/app.config.json updated with the bootstrapAudit section

This is intended to help me quickly assess Bootstrap usage and template linkage for an AngularJS codebase, so please make sure the extraction and counts reflect the actual contents of the provided files. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
